examples: The origin of multicellular forms of life seems a relatively simple step compared to the origin of life itself.
2-billion-year-old Fossils May Be Earliest Known Multicellular life.
Some species gather by the thousands to form multicellular bodies that can crawl.
That is not surprising, since cancer (the uncontrolled multiplication of cells) is a problem unique to multicellular organisms.
What genetic change allowed multicellular animals to come into existence remains obscure.
